{"id":"hyperopen-fq5","title":"Account mode parity: unified vs standard account value rendering","description":"Observed parity gap: Hyperopen treats `dexAbstraction` accounts as unified, which causes standard accounts to render unified-style account value and balance composition.

Reference addresses validated on 2026-03-04 via trade.xyz ghost mode:
- Unified: `0x4096d3377ae5ade578daae8188804740c8b1da3e`
- Standard (DEX abstraction): `0x2ba553d9f990a3b66b03b2dc0d030dfc1c061036`

Evidence summary:
- trade.xyz unified address shows `Unified Account Value` and unified-style balance treatment.
- trade.xyz standard address shows `Account Value` and split `Spot` + `Perps` equity treatment.
- Hyperliquid `userAbstraction` returns:
  - unified address => `"unifiedAccount"`
  - standard address => `"dexAbstraction"`
- Hyperopen currently normalizes `"dexAbstraction"` to `:unified` in `/hyperopen/src/hyperopen/api/endpoints/account.cljs`.

Goal:
Implement account-mode detection and rendering parity so:
1. Unified accounts render unified account value semantics.
2. Standard accounts render standard account value semantics.
3. Ghost mode and owner-mode both use the same deterministic mode-resolution path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Account mode projection distinguishes at least `:unified` and `:standard` (or equivalent semantic split) for Hyperliquid abstraction payloads.
- Portfolio summary, account-equity panel, and balances projection respect the mode split.
- Regression tests cover both provided addresses as fixture snapshots for mode-sensitive rendering outputs.
- Required gates pass: `npm run check`, `npm test`, `npm run test:websocket`.

{"id":"hyperopen-918","title":"Reduce spotClearinghouseState fanout under WS user ledger cadence","description":"Manual browser QA on 2026-03-05 against branch-local build (http://127.0.0.1:8081) found that after ghost-mode activation for 0x162cc7c861ebd0c06b3d72319201150482518185, account streams are subscribed with status :n-a (openOrders/webData2/userFills/userFundings/userNonFundingLedgerUpdates), and openOrders fallback POSTs are suppressed as expected by sf6 fix.\\n\\nResidual issue: spotClearinghouseState POSTs continue at high cadence (~6-7 calls per ~8s observed) via websocket.user refresh loop stack:\\nwebsocket.user eval line -\u003e refresh_account_surfaces_after_user_fill_BANG_ -\u003e refresh_spot_clearinghouse_snapshot_BANG_ -\u003e api default request_spot_clearinghouse_state_BANG_ -\u003e post_info_BANG_.\\n\\nImpact: while frontendOpenOrders and default clearinghouse fallback are fixed, high-frequency spotClearinghouseState can still contribute to /info pressure and rate-limit risk for active accounts.\\n\\nScope proposal:\\n- Add debounce/TTL/dedupe guard for spotClearinghouseState in user fill/ledger refresh path.\\n- Gate by stream/event freshness and avoid repeated high-priority refresh storms.\\n- Add regression test for repeated user ledger updates proving bounded spot POST cadence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"thegeronimo@protonmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-05T15:06:52Z","created_by":"Geronimo","updated_at":"2026-03-05T15:06:52Z"}
